signals.title: NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: NZD/JPY at 113.307 — NZD is the strongest G10 performer (avg +1.284%) while JPY remains the laggard. Despite NZD COT LF net short -27,675 (18% long), the RBNZ 2.5% [HIKING] stance vs BoJ 1.0% [HIKING] creates a +150bp nominal carry advantage for NZD. RBNZ 2.5%.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.403 — Pair remains bid on +275bp Fed-BoJ carry (SOFR 3.64% vs TONA 0.98%). However, US-Iran tensions and a bear steepener regime (+87.2bp 2s10s) introduce tail risk. Options market is pricing protection: IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.6% (VRP +3.6%) with a moderate -1.37 25d RR put skew. JPY COT LF net short -104,231 is crowded; intervention risk rises on a sustained hold above 160.00.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1443 — ECB 2.25% [HIKING] vs Fed 3.75% [ON HOLD] maintains a -150bp carry deficit for EUR. CESI data shows a strong negative bias for EUR (net -15) vs USD (net +51), suggesting macro fundamentals favor USD. COT LF net short -66,057 (34.7% long) aligns with the data trend. Watch for the final Eurozone CPI report today to confirm if ECB 'September hike' bets remain viable.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Vol Premium + Retail Divergence
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3449 — Options market shows extreme hedging demand with IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.8% (VRP +4.2%). While COT LF net long +17,228 (59% long) suggests institutional support, retail is 61% short. Carry is neutral (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%), but the UK 10Y at 5.01% vs US 10Y at 4.57% provides a yield cushion. Watch for a break of the near-term range as noted by UOB.

signals.title: AUD/CHF — Retail Extreme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: AUD/CHF at 0.5630 — Retail sentiment is at an extreme 89% short, diverging from the +435bp carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs SNB 0.0%). AUD COT LF net long +24,098 (63.8% long) confirms institutional long bias. Retail capitulation risk is high if AUD/CHF holds above recent lows, though AUD CESI (net -13) remains a headwind. Watch the next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug for carry sustainability.
